round3_status: FINAL re-confirmation. Whole-shape decidable:false has now been argued with exhaustive per-tool reasoning in rounds 0, 1, and 2, and is RE-CONFIRMED here in round 3 after re-reading bench/tool_registry.json. The registry exposes exactly four BUILT descriptors (corpus_queue:byte_freq_ratio, seed_set:struct_size_and_token_count, seed_set:value_distance_reached, seed_set:depth_reach) and the data_realities expose NO per-seed energy / campaign-dynamics source. Every one was already accounted for by descriptor key in the round-2 re-examination. No missed signal exists. No mechanism invented to force a label (G3).

round0_empirical_result.summary: Round-0 proposed two terminal-corpus hypotheses (calibrated_structural_assembly via struct_size_and_token_count; calibrated_rare_value_targeting via value_distance_reached). The arbiter scored all 13 branches (harfbuzz 6, sqlite3 3, libxml2 3, curl 1) and returned rule_not_met on every one. 0 branches ever labeled across all rounds.
round0_empirical_result.interpretation: The arbiter did NOT find size_lift>=1.3 / tag_lift>=1.3 (assembly) nor value_dist_lift>1 (rare-value) on any decidable member. This empirically confirms the scheduling_caveat: a terminal seed-set snapshot does not separate calibrated from naive.

why_decidable_false.core: calibrated_energy (minimizer / CalibrationStage perf_score) is a SCHEDULING / POWER technique. Its only mechanistic difference from naive is the per-seed havoc TRIAL COUNT allocated over campaign time (energy = perf_score x visits). The mutator vocabulary is byte-identical across all 4 fuzzers (data_realities #1) and coverage-feedback admission is identical, so the advantage is a campaign-DYNAMICS property (how much energy a seed received before terminal), not a property of the terminal saved corpus.
why_decidable_false.tools_are_terminal_only: All four BUILT measurement tools read TERMINAL corpus / seed-set state: corpus_queue:byte_freq_ratio (corpus byte-freq), seed_set:struct_size_and_token_count (seed-set size+tokens), seed_set:value_distance_reached (seed-set Hamming distance to literal), seed_set:depth_reach (per-seed exec count of the branch line). None observes per-seed energy-over-time. A terminal snapshot reports WHAT the corpus contains, never HOW MUCH scheduling energy produced it.
why_decidable_false.equal_admission_kills_the_signal: Because calibrated and naive admit seeds by the SAME coverage rule with the SAME mutators, naive's corpus eventually accumulates structurally-similar and value-equally-close seeds to calibrated's -- just produced later / slower. Every signature's mechanism_summary attributes the win to 'more havoc iterations / extra mutation budget / raises the probability', i.e. faster-via-more-energy, NOT a corpus content the naive corpus cannot also reach. Therefore size_lift ~ 1.0 and value_dist_lift ~ 1.0 are the EXPECTED terminal readings even when the live scheduling advantage was real. The arbiter's all-rule_not_met result is exactly that prediction, observed.
why_decidable_false.g2_independence_is_unsatisfiable: The only terminal observable that WOULD separate calibrated from naive (calibrated's corpus reached the deep gate, naive's did not) is the resolve outcome itself -- which is the label-SOURCE, not an independent verification-SIGNAL. Verifying 'calibrated reaches deeper' with 'calibrated resolved' violates guardrail G2. There is no terminal observable independent of the resolve pattern for a pure power-schedule effect.
why_decidable_false.the_discriminating_signal_is_unbuilt_and: A real discriminator would be a per-seed energy/dynamics descriptor (compute energy_share / plateau_time over source=seed_lineage|exec_stats|coverage_timeseries) -- e.g. cumulative havoc execs per seed, or time-to-plateau per arm. No such tool exists in tool_registry.json (re-verified round 3: only corpus_queue:byte_freq_ratio, seed_set:struct_size_and_token_count, seed_set:value_distance_reached, seed_set:depth_reach are built) and the registry exposes no per-seed energy accounting. Even a built energy_share tool would be confounded: a higher energy share on the calibrated side is what the scheduling technique DOES by definition, so it re-states the technique rather than independently verifying the per-branch mechanism, again straining G2.
why_decidable_false.depth_reach_explicitly_excluded: depth_reach (seed_set:depth_reach:branch_line:per_branch) runs the WINNER-RESOLVING seed set vs the LOSER-BLOCKING seed set through the cov binary and reports winner_branch_taken / loser_branch_taken / depth_lift. For this shape winner=calibrated(minimizer, resolving), loser=naive(blocking). Its core prediction (winner_branch_taken AND NOT loser_branch_taken, depth_lift>>1) is TAUTOLOGICAL with the resolve label: resolving seeds take the gate's true side and blocking seeds do not BY DEFINITION of resolving/blocking, so 'winner_deeper' simply re-encodes 'calibrated resolved, naive blocked' -- a direct G2 violation. depth_reach also has NO per-seed energy axis, so it cannot separate the scheduling mechanism (calibrated got more energy/time) from a content alternative (calibrated assembled deeper structure) -- both yield identical line-reaching terminal corpora. It therefore adds no INDEPENDENT discrimination for a pure power-schedule technique and is correctly excluded.

merges_and_collapses.round0_hypotheses_dropped: Both round-0 hypotheses (calibrated_structural_assembly, calibrated_rare_value_targeting) are dropped. They were not refuted as WRONG mechanism stories -- the signatures' attributions (more energy -> deeper structure / higher byte-flip probability) are plausible -- but they are NON-DISCRIMINABLE from naive with any BUILT terminal-corpus tool, which the arbiter's all-rule_not_met outcome confirms. Per job 2 + guardrail G3, an honest non-discriminable verdict beats a rule the data cannot support.
merges_and_collapses.no_subgroup_survives: Neither the structural-assembly subgroup (8 members) nor the rare-value subgroup (3 members) yields a discriminable terminal signal: both subgroups attribute the win to per-seed energy/trial-count, which equal-admission + identical-mutators makes invisible in the terminal corpus. No sub-shape is rescued.

round3_reexamination: Round-3 (FINAL) brief required confirming whole-shape decidable:false UNLESS a concrete BUILT-tool-measurable G2-independent discriminating signal was missed in BOTH prior rounds. Re-read bench/tool_registry.json: only four built descriptors exist (corpus_queue:byte_freq_ratio:target_vs_decoy, seed_set:struct_size_and_token_count, seed_set:value_distance_reached, seed_set:depth_reach), and data_realities expose NO per-seed energy / exec-count-over-time / plateau source. All four were already enumerated and excluded by descriptor key in round 2 (byte/size/value computes are terminal-corpus measures already shown non-discriminable and rule_not_met in round-0; depth_reach is a G2 tautology with no energy axis). No tool, source, or compute was missed. CONCLUSION: no missed signal. Whole-shape decidable:false RE-CONFIRMED for the third and final time. This is convergence (G3), not a re-design failure.
data_realities_respected: Did NOT propose any seed_lineage 'I2S event' or 'gradient event' descriptor (data_realities #1, #2) -- the would-be energy/lineage discriminator is unbuilt and would target campaign dynamics, not a tagged mutation_op event.
Did NOT propose byte_freq_ratio on the structural-assembly members (data_realities #3) -- those gates have different-sized winning vs blocking seeds.

Marked libxml2 members decidable:false on MECHANISM grounds (scheduling-only) that hold for ALL servers, NOT merely because libxml2 lacks an on-disk corpus on s4 (the owning server arbitrates its own targets, rule #4). The no-corpus note is recorded as a secondary, not the primary, reason.
notes: WHOLE-SHAPE decidable:false (honest non-discriminable), RE-CONFIRMED in round-3 (FINAL) after re-reading every built registry descriptor. This shape has now been argued non-discriminable in rounds 0, 1, and 2 with exhaustive per-tool reasoning; round 3 finds no signal both prior rounds missed. calibrated_energy is a power-SCHEDULING technique whose advantage is a campaign-DYNAMICS quantity (per-seed energy x time), NOT a property of the terminal saved corpus that every built tool reads. Equal coverage-admission + byte-identical mutators mean naive's terminal corpus reaches structurally/value-comparable seeds (just slower), so the round-0 terminal-corpus measurements read no calibrated-vs-naive lift -- the arbiter returned rule_not_met on all 13 branches (harfbuzz/sqlite3/libxml2/curl), empirically confirming the scheduling_caveat. The only would-be discriminator is a per-seed energy/plateau descriptor (over seed_lineage|exec_stats|coverage_timeseries) which is (a) UNBUILT and absent from tool_registry.json, and (b) would re-state the technique rather than independently verify it, straining G2. This is the spec's non-local / non-synthesizable scheduling case (CLAUDE.md benchmark-pivot: scheduling is a whole-target+campaign property); an honest decidable:false here is the CORRECT, paper-relevant outcome (guardrail G3), not a failure. All 13 branches route to fallback:inconclusive. NO hypothesis was invented to force labels.
